Name: Huginn, Thought
Effect: On enter: Look at the top 3 cards of your deck. Put them back in any order.
Anecdote: 'Over Middle-earth he flies each morning and returns at the day-meal with news for the Allfather's ear.'

Name: Muninn, Memory
Effect: On enter: Return a card from your underworld to your hand.
Anecdote: 'For Huginn I fear, that he come not home — yet more anxious am I for Muninn.'

Name: Völva, the Seeress
Effect: On enter: Look at the top 2 cards of your deck. Put one into your hand and the other at the bottom.
Anecdote: 'Much I know, said the seeress. I see further — beyond the doom of the gods. Would you know yet more?'

Name: Ratatoskr, the Gossip
Effect: On enter: All players reveal their hands.
Anecdote: 'Up and down the ash Yggdrasil he runs, carrying spiteful words between the eagle above and the serpent below.'

Name: Mimir's Well
Effect: On enter: You may banish one of your beings to draw three cards.
Anecdote: 'A drink from the well is not given for nothing. The Allfather left his eye in the water, and it lies there still.'

Name: Mimir's Severed Head
Effect: While on top: At the end of your turn, you may look at the top card of your deck and put it at the bottom.
Anecdote: 'The Vanir sent back his head. Odin smeared it with herbs and sang songs over it, and it speaks to him of hidden things.'

Name: Gunnlod, Keeper of the Mead
Effect: While on top: Once per turn, you may pay [1] to draw a card.
Anecdote: 'Three nights he lay beside her in the mountain, and she granted him three sips of the precious mead. Each sip emptied a vat.'

Name: Geri and Freki
Anecdote: 'The war-wont father of hosts feeds his two wolves all the meat from his table; wine alone is food and drink to Odin.'

Name: Sleipnir, the Eight-Legged
Effect: While on top: Once per turn, you may move Sleipnir and the card beneath it to another location.
Anecdote: 'Eight legs bear him over sea and sky, the best of horses, grey as storm — Loki's strangest child.'

Name: Odin, the Wanderer
Effect: While on top: At the end of your turn, if 'Huginn, Thought' and 'Muninn, Memory' are in play, draw a card.
Anecdote: 'A one-eyed old man in a blue cloak and a broad hat asked the king for a hearing. His questions were sharper than they seemed.'

Name: Nine Nights on the Tree
Effect: On enter: You may destroy a friendly being. If you do, draw cards equal to its cost.
Anecdote: 'I know that I hung on the wind-torn tree nine whole nights, wounded by the spear, given to Odin — myself to myself.'

Name: Vafthrudnir, the Riddle-Giant
Effect: On enter: Look at the top card of an opponent's deck. You may put it at the bottom of their deck.
Anecdote: 'Wager your head, guest, on the wisdom-game. — Doomed was the giant when the last question came: What did Odin whisper in the ear of his dead son on the pyre?'

Name: Suttungr, the Mead's Warden
Effect: While on top: Opponents cannot draw cards outside their draw step.
Anecdote: 'He hid the mead in the heart of the mountain and set his daughter to guard it, and shared not a drop with god or man.'

Name: Kvasir, Born of All Gods
Effect: On enter: Draw two cards. On destruction: Draw 'The Mead of Poetry' from your deck.
Anecdote: 'Made from the spittle of every god, he wandered the world answering all questions, until two dwarves grew tired of wisdom and cut his throat.'

Name: The Mead of Poetry
Anecdote: 'Whoever drinks of it becomes a poet or a scholar. Odin drank all three vats in three draughts and flew home as an eagle, spilling a little behind him.'
